summary: A Go-based monolithic runner and MCP handler system heavily utilizing automated agent workflows for extensive test generation and validation.
concerns: High volume of 'extra-tests' agent sessions suggests potential test flakiness or coverage gaps
Staged but uncommitted changes (1 staged) could lead to drift
Heavy reliance on automated test generation agents might obscure actual business logic quality
Scattered test directories across multiple agent sessions indicate fragmented test management
suggestions: Consolidate the numerous 'extra-tests' agent outputs into a unified, maintainable test suite
Review and merge the currently staged changes to prevent context loss
Focus on the core runner and MCP handler logic rather than generating peripheral tests
Establish clear boundaries for when to use automated test agents vs. manual test authoring
